bindings.quest_action: gather evidence of the commander's corruption and rally support
bindings.quest_focus: the commander's private stockpile of diverted supplies
bindings.quest_stakes: the people scraping by on scraps while one person hoards
bindings.quest_threat: a corrupt commander who threatens exile for anyone who speaks up
bindings.quest_origin: months of supply diversion and intimidation

dialogueChunks.intro: Keep your voice down, {player_name}. The walls have ears these days, and not all of them are friendly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} has been hoarding supplies while {quest_stakes}. {target_npc} caught them diverting {quest_item} to {quest_focus} {location_hint}. When confronted, the commander doubled the guard and threatened anyone who speaks up. We need someone from outside to {quest_action}: someone who can't be intimidated or banished.
dialogueChunks.outro: We built this settlement together. Every brick, every beam. And now one person acts like they own it all. That ends now.

playerResponses.text: Hoarding while people starve? I'll help you expose the truth.
Political disputes aren't my specialty, but corruption is corruption. I'm in.
Internal power struggles are a hornet's nest. I'd rather not get stung.

bindings.quest_action: stockpile enough supplies to sustain the settlement independently
bindings.quest_focus: the unjust tax draining every family dry
bindings.quest_stakes: farmers breaking their backs while soldiers eat like kings
bindings.quest_threat: a garrison commander who taxes a third of everything we produce
bindings.quest_origin: a tax imposed last month that pushed us past the breaking point

dialogueChunks.intro: You picked a hell of a time to visit, {player_name}. Things are about to change around here.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat}: {quest_focus} goes to the guard barracks. {quest_stakes} while the soldiers feast. {target_npc} tried to petition for relief and got thrown in the stocks. Half the settlement is ready to refuse the tax, but we need {quest_item} to {quest_action} and hold the line when troops come to collect.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not rebels, {player_name}. We're people who've been pushed past the breaking point. Will you stand with us?

playerResponses.text: A third of everything? That's not a tax, it's theft. Count me in.
Standing against armed soldiers is risky. What's your plan?
I don't pick sides in local politics. Sort this out yourselves.

bindings.quest_action: establish an independent trading post beyond the consortium's reach
bindings.quest_focus: the trade monopoly that controls who buys and sells
bindings.quest_stakes: every independent trader crushed by rigged prices
bindings.quest_threat: a merchant consortium that confiscates goods and burns stalls
bindings.quest_origin: years of price-fixing and suppression of free trade

dialogueChunks.intro: I'm going to tell you something dangerous, {player_name}. Dangerous for me and dangerous for you, just by hearing it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} controls all trade here. They set prices, decide who buys, and anyone who trades independently has their goods seized. {target_npc} had their entire inventory of {quest_item} confiscated for undercutting the consortium. A group of us want to {quest_action} {location_hint}, but the route is infested with {enemy_type} and we need supplies to build it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Freedom to trade shouldn't require permission from {quest_focus}. Help us build something they can't control.

playerResponses.text: A free trading post? That's a cause worth fighting for.
Breaking a trade monopoly sounds profitable for everyone. I'm listening.
Cross a merchant consortium? Those people have long memories and deep pockets. No thanks.

bindings.quest_action: expose the warden's deal and free the forced laborers
bindings.quest_focus: the secret labor deal with hostile creatures
bindings.quest_stakes: workers being forced into enemy territory under threat
bindings.quest_threat: a warden selling our people's labor to the enemy
bindings.quest_origin: a betrayal discovered when workers were followed to enemy overseers

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to ask you something, and I need you to decide quickly. Before the patrol comes back around.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat}: sending workers into {enemy_type} territory to mine {quest_item} in exchange for personal protection. {target_npc} discovered {quest_focus} when they followed a work crew {location_hint} and saw them handing over ore to enemy overseers. {quest_stakes} are terrified: the warden has threatened their families. We need proof, allies, and to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Our own leader is trading our people's sweat and safety for personal gain. This betrayal cannot stand.

playerResponses.text: Selling labor to hostile creatures? That's slavery with a middleman. I'll help you end it.
This is bigger than a revolt: this is liberation. Tell me the plan.
Going against a warden with {enemy_type} allies? The odds are terrible.
